Discord Role Bot Tutorial: Auto-Assign Roles via Invite Links (Invite-Role Bot)

When you want new members to automatically get a specific role based on which invite link they used, vanilla Discord can't do it on its own — you need a bot. This post walks through using Invite-Role Bot to wire invite links to roles.

CodeSandbox is genuinely great — and pretty different from CodePen. Where CodePen is "online editor for snippets", CodeSandbox is more like "your local dev environment, in the cloud" — full templates for React / Vue / Angular / Node / static, real npm install support, and a UI that's 87% VSCode. This post walks through the basics: creating a sandbox, installing packages, keyboard shortcuts, and exporting back to a local project.

Quick note on what robots.txt actually does, why it matters for SEO, and how to write one. Covers the basic directives (User-agent, Disallow, Allow, Sitemap), the most common crawler User-agents, and the important caveat — robots.txt is a convention, not a guarantee, so don't rely on it to hide sensitive info.
Homebrew: homebrew-core / homebrew-cask Is a Shallow Clone — How to Fix It

Recently when installing Homebrew, I started seeing "homebrew-core is a shallow clone." and "homebrew-cask is a shallow clone." errors during install. This post records the fix — two git fetch --unshallow commands you need to run manually to make the install complete.
Window Went Off-Screen on Mac or Windows? How to Pull It Back

Sometimes a window drifts off your screen and you can't grab it with the mouse. This post covers two quick fixes — Alt + Space → Move + arrow keys on Windows to drag any off-screen window back, and the Window → Zoom menu (or force-quit) on Mac.
Mac: "zsh: command not found: xxx" — Two Common Causes and Fixes

Hit "zsh: command not found: hexo" or similar on Mac after installing a global npm package? Two flavors of this issue, both common — one if you use nvm (npm prefix conflicting with nvm), one if you don't (zsh PATH not picking up npm's global bin directory). This post covers both fixes step by step.
Core Web Vitals for SEO: LCP, FID, and CLS

Heads-up that this post is from 2021 — Google replaced FID with INP in March 2024. The general framing still applies. Originally written when I noticed my Google Search Console flagging my site with bad "page experience" scores — turns out Google's Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) had been quietly added in 2020 and started factoring into SEO. This post is a quick tour of the three metrics, their thresholds, and how to test your site.
SPA, CSR, SSR, MPA, SSG — A Quick Take on Frontend Terminology

Quick walkthrough of the terms that come up everywhere in frontend job interviews — SSR (Server Side Render), MPA (Multi-Page Application), CSR (Client Side Render), SPA (Single-page Application), and SSG (Static Site Generation). Includes a postman-and-painter analogy to make the request flow click, plus how to tell SSR vs. CSR from a site's "view source".
Software Versioning: What Alpha, Beta, and RC Actually Mean

Quick reference for the version-stage names you see everywhere — Pre-alpha, Alpha, Beta, Release Candidate (RC), and the Latest-release / Pre-release tags on GitHub. Covers when it's safe-ish to use each in real projects, and the difference between open source and closed source conventions (Microsoft's RC vs. Apple's "Golden Master").
Can I Use? A Five-Minute Guide to Checking CSS / HTML / JS Browser Support

Frontend devs use new CSS / HTML / JS features all the time — sometimes without realizing they're not supported in every browser. Can I Use is the go-to support-checking service. This post is a five-minute tour of using it — searching for features, reading the support matrix and color legend, the vendor-prefix tooling, and a quick aside on what CSS prefixes are and when they matter.
